_source: Constructed from the Claude Agent SDK message types (SystemMessage, AssistantMessage, ResultMessage, etc.) documented at code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk and from the actual message iteration pattern shown in the SDK Python repo. Anthropic does not publish a per-customer sample trace, but the SDK emits structured messages that can be logged for auditing.

messages.content: I need to look up the order first, verify the customer's claim, then issue the refund if policy permits.
messages._note: If thinking is enabled (anthropic.com extended thinking), reasoning blocks appear before tool calls
PreToolUse hook ran before tool execution. For read-only tools it allows; for issue_refund below it will check policy.
The PreToolUse hook blocked execution and the SDK now waits for user / approver decision.

input.question: Refund of $245.00 to customer requires approval. Approve?

messages._note: AskUserQuestion is a first-class built-in tool. The SDK pauses and surfaces the question to the caller.
After approval, issue_refund executed and returned successfully

messages.result: Refund of $245.00 has been processed for order ORD-77129834. Customer will see the credit in 3-5 business days.

_session_persistence._implication: Buyer can replay or branch any agent run. Forking is a first-class operation: graph.fork_session() in the SDK.

_caveats_for_procurement: Trace SHAPE is real — extracted from the Agent SDK message types.
Specific tool names and arguments are illustrative — Anthropic does not publish per-customer sample traces.
PreToolUse / PostToolUse hooks are an Anthropic-specific differentiator vs OpenAI; the trace shows real hook-driven flow control.
AskUserQuestion as a built-in tool is unusual — most SDKs leave user interaction as a developer-implemented integration.
